_comment: MOCKUP/DESIGN data. Quoted from the public IRDAI 2026 ICSG (Annexure-III audit checklist) document (see source link), re-verified 2026-06-12. CORRECTION vs prior belief: the PQC item is REAL in the 2026 revision (it was absent in 2023). It is an AUDIT-CHECKLIST item (auditors will ask it), numbered row 110 sequentially — NOT a regulator-branded code 'Control 110'. The PQC wording lives ONLY in the Annexure-III checklist, not the 175-page guideline body.
title: IRDAI Information & Cybersecurity Guidelines, 2026 — Cryptographic-Asset Inventory
summary: IRDAI's 2026 cybersecurity guidelines add an audit-checklist question asking insurers whether they maintain a cryptographic-asset inventory for post-quantum readiness — exactly what CryptaMap produces. It is principle-level (no algorithm/key-size/TLS named) and lives in the auditor's checklist, so the obligation is 'be ready to answer yes, with evidence'. CryptaMap is that evidence for the AWS surface.

documentsHeld.title: IRDAI Information and Cybersecurity Guidelines, 2026 — Annexure-III Auditor's Report Checklist (full)
IRDAI Information and Cybersecurity Guidelines, 2026 — main guideline body (full, 175 pp)

claims.headline: Audit checklist asks: do you maintain a cryptographic-asset inventory for post-quantum readiness?
claims.quote: Does the organization maintain an up-to-date inventory of its cryptographic assets to ensure preparedness for transition to post-quantum cryptographic environments?
claims.where: Annexure-III Auditor's Report Checklist, Asset Management (ID.AM) category, row 110
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap is precisely the 'up-to-date inventory of cryptographic assets' this audit item asks for, and it surfaces post-quantum readiness (quantum-resistant vs not) per asset. Scope honesty: CryptaMap covers the AWS-managed-service crypto surface; the auditor's question spans the whole organisation. It is also an audit-CHECKLIST item (auditors verify it), not a numbered statutory control.

honestCaveats: This is row 110 in the Annexure-III AUDIT CHECKLIST — auditors will ask it. It is NOT a regulator-branded code called 'IRDAI Control 110' (that earlier framing was inaccurate).

The post-quantum wording appears ONLY in the audit checklist (Annexure-III), not in the 175-page guideline body. The guideline names no specific algorithm, key size, or TLS version — it is a maintain-inventory + PQ-readiness intent.

CryptaMap covers the AWS-managed-service crypto surface, not the insurer's entire IT estate, and is evidence, not a compliance certification. Verify against the latest IRDAI guideline before audit use.
